codeReview.summary: This fix does not clear the prior VAL-CROSS-004 blocker. The reviewed worker session is a proof-audit only: commit `1ce5893` adds a single shared proof bundle owned by the superseded feature `mission-cross-flow-feature-owned-proof-followup`, and the daemon-proof handoff explicitly says no new code changes were required for this feature. The cited runtime-controls artifact proves real daemon failure -> retry -> pause chronology, but the continue-via-chat portion is still inferred from focused tests plus an isolated paused-state renderer setup rather than new same-session Mission-GUI-TEST proof owned by `mission-cross-flow-daemon-failure-continuation-proof`.

issues.description: The handoff closes this feature by pointing at `.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json` and stating that no new code changes were required because HEAD already contained the deliverable. But the feature contract requires `mission-cross-flow-daemon-failure-continuation-proof` to own new checked-in proof for VAL-CROSS-004, and this session added no daemon-specific artifact or newly-owned evidence for that feature.

The only checked-in proof bundle cited for VAL-CROSS-004 declares `"featureId": "mission-cross-flow-feature-owned-proof-followup"`, while mission metadata says that follow-up feature is superseded and retained only as supporting context. Even though the bundle maps `VAL-CROSS-004` at line 75, it is not owned by `mission-cross-flow-daemon-failure-continuation-proof`, so it does not satisfy the requirement for feature-owned proof for this narrower daemon-recovery feature.

The artifact reused as interactive proof still records that unsafe runtime-control permutations were validated with isolated in-memory renderer-state injection plus focused tests because the live Mission-GUI-TEST run was treated as read-only. For VAL-CROSS-004, that means the real Electron evidence covers daemon failure -> retry -> `mission_paused`, but the claimed continue-via-normal-chat behavior is still derived from supporting focused tests and injected paused-state evidence rather than a new same-session Mission-GUI-TEST daemon-failure continuation proof.

summary: Scrutiny failed. The daemon-recovery fix session only revalidated commit `1ce5893`'s shared follow-up bundle and did not add feature-owned proof for `mission-cross-flow-daemon-failure-continuation-proof`; blocking evidence for same-session daemon/factoryd failure -> continue via normal chat still depends on a superseded owner mapping plus injected-state-assisted runtime-controls validation.
